questionHtml: Describe how ATP is produced by Photosystem II in the light-dependent stage of photosynthesis.
markschemeHtml:
1. light (energy) absorbed by pigments/chlorophyll/photosystems;
  2. excited electrons passed to electron carriers/electron transport chain;
  3. protons/hydrogen ions pumped into thylakoid (space);
  4. proton gradient/high proton concentration generated;
  5. protons pass via ATP synthase to the stroma;
  6. ATP synthase phosphorylates ADP/ATP synthase converts ADP to ATP;
  7. photophosphorylation/chemiosmosis;
  8. ATP synthase/electron carriers/proton pumps/photosystems/pigment are in the thylakoid membrane;

meta.title: Describe how ATP is produced by Photosystem II in the light-dependent stage of photosynthesis.
